items.passage: Many plants rely on animals to spread their seeds far from the parent plant. Birds and small mammals often eat fruit and later drop the undigested seeds in new locations. Some seeds attach to fur or feathers using small hooks that catch on passing animals. Wind and water also carry lightweight seeds across long distances, sometimes between islands. This variety of methods helps plants colonize new habitats and reduce competition with their parents.

Wildlife reserves protect threatened species by preserving large stretches of their natural habitat. Park rangers patrol these areas to prevent illegal hunting and monitor population numbers carefully. Conservation biologists also breed endangered animals in captivity before releasing them back into protected zones. Local communities sometimes benefit from tourism that visits these reserves and brings important income. However, climate change continues to threaten even well-managed reserves through shifting temperatures and altered rainfall patterns.

Psychologists distinguish two main types of motivation that drive human behavior. Intrinsic motivation comes from internal satisfaction, such as curiosity or the joy of mastering a new skill. Extrinsic motivation depends on external rewards like money, grades, or recognition from others. Research suggests that intrinsic motivation tends to produce more lasting engagement than rewards offered from outside. However, well-timed external rewards can still help people get started on tasks they would otherwise avoid.

The Industrial Revolution transformed work patterns across Britain during the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries. Factories employed children as young as six, who worked long shifts in dangerous conditions for very low wages. Reformers gradually pushed Parliament to pass laws limiting working hours and requiring basic education for young workers. The Factory Acts of the 1830s and 1840s set minimum ages and reduced shifts in textile mills. However, enforcement remained uneven throughout much of the century.

The Chicxulub impact occurred about sixty-six million years ago near the modern Yucatán coast. The asteroid was roughly ten kilometers across and struck Earth with enormous force, leaving a crater nearly two hundred kilometers wide. Massive amounts of dust and ash spread through the atmosphere, blocking sunlight for many years afterward. This sudden darkness caused widespread plant collapse and contributed to the mass extinction of dinosaurs. Today, scientists study the buried crater using deep drill cores and seismic surveys.

Cosmic rays are high-energy particles that travel through space at nearly the speed of light. Most originate from supernova explosions in distant parts of our galaxy and beyond. When these particles strike the upper atmosphere, they collide with air molecules and produce showers of secondary particles. Detectors on the ground and on satellites measure these showers to study their original sources. However, the highest-energy cosmic rays remain difficult to trace because their paths bend in galactic magnetic fields.
